context.meal: breaded fish bites, roasted potatoes, tartar sauce
context.setting: Watching TV with TV in Front

episode_text:
day=15; meal=breaded fish bites, roasted potatoes, tartar sauce; setting=Watching TV with TV in Front; time_of_day=morning;
preferences: microwave_time=3 min; occlusion_relevance=minimize front occlusion; robot_speed=slow; skewering_axis=perpendicular to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=yes; transfer_mode=inside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=not applicable; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=speech + LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=autocontinue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=autocontinue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=speech + LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=autocontinue; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; retract_between_bites=yes; bite_dipping_preference=less; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=10 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a simple savory meal without sauces or dips THEN 1 min BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected the baseline reheating time in straightforward front-TV savory meals. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a sauced mixed dish, fish bites with tartar sauce, or another more substantial warm meal with a dip/sauce THEN 3 min BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected longer reheating in front-TV meals when the meal was more substantial or sauce-associated. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is bite-sized sandwiches THEN 3 min BECAUSE the user previously selected longer reheating for that front-TV lunch type. IF dining context includes working on a laptop in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a warm savory meal with a dip or sauce such as ketchup THEN 2 min BECAUSE the user selected an intermediate reheating time in the observed front-laptop dipped lunch. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left OR working on a laptop on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food includes sauce-bearing, dip, dessert, or bite-sized sandwich items served warm THEN 3 min BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected longer reheating in observed left-attention meals with such foods. IF dining context includes a social partner on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a warm sauced savory entree THEN 2 min BECAUSE the user selected moderate reheating rather than maximal reheating in the observed left-social lunch. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is fresh fruit intended to be served cold THEN no microwave BECAUSE the user selected no microwaving for a left-TV fruit breakfast. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right OR working on a laptop on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is cold fruit, fruit salad, or another cold-served item THEN no microwave BECAUSE the user selected no microwaving in both right-attention cold-fruit meals. IF dining context includes a social partner on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a mixed breakfast-dessert plate with bananas, brownies, or chocolate sauce THEN 1 min BECAUSE the user selected light reheating in the observed right-social morning meal.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes a social partner in front of the user AND affective state is neutral AND food has no special sauce/dip relevance THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user explicitly chose front-occlusion minimization in a front-social context. IF dining context includes watching TV in front OR working on a laptop in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is simple, dipped, or sauced THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose to preserve the forward view whenever an important visual target was directly ahead, including the new front-TV dipped savory meal. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left OR working on a laptop on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food includes sauce-bearing, dip, dessert, cold fruit, or bite-sized sandwich items THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose left-occlusion minimization whenever the important visual target was on the left. IF dining context includes a social partner on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a warm savory entree with sauce THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE the user selected left-occlusion minimization in the observed left-social meal. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right OR working on a laptop on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food includes cold fruit, dessert, or other simple snack-like items THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user selected right-occlusion minimization in both observed right-attention meals. IF dining context includes a social partner on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food includes breakfast-dessert items such as bananas, brownies, or chocolate sauce THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user selected right-occlusion minimization in the observed right-social meal.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a simple savory meal without sauces or dips THEN fast BECAUSE the user selected the fastest speed in repeated front-TV meals with straightforward foods. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a sauced mixed dish THEN medium BECAUSE the user slowed the robot for a messier mixed entree. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is bite-sized sandwiches OR a dipped savory meal such as fish bites with tartar sauce THEN slow BECAUSE the user selected the slowest speed in those front-TV meals, indicating some hand-held or dip-associated foods prompt maximum caution. IF dining context includes working on a laptop in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a warm savory meal with a dip or sauce such as ketchup THEN medium BECAUSE the user selected medium speed in the observed front-laptop dipped lunch. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left OR working on a laptop on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food includes sauce, dip, dessert, or cold fruit THEN slow BECAUSE left-attention contexts repeatedly show a preference for slower motion. IF dining context includes a social partner on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a warm sauced savory entree THEN medium BECAUSE the user selected medium speed in the observed left-social meal. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right OR working on a laptop on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a light cold fruit or dessert item THEN fast BECAUSE the user selected fast speed in both observed right-attention cold-fruit meals. IF dining context includes a social partner on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food includes bananas, brownies, or chocolate sauce THEN fast BECAUSE the user selected fast speed in the observed right-social meal.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes watching TV in front OR watching TV on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is bite-sized sandwiches THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the user selected parallel skewering in those meals, suggesting a preference for narrower bites for sandwich pieces. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is cut fruit pieces such as cantaloupe, banana, or watermelon THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the user selected parallel skewering in the observed left-TV fruit meal. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a simple savory meal, a dipped savory meal such as fish bites with tartar sauce, or a sauced mixed dish other than sandwich pieces THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user selected this option across repeated front-facing savory meals, including the new dipped fish meal, reflecting a general preference for acquisition reliability unless a narrower bite seems desirable. IF dining context includes working on a laptop in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a savory meal with a simple dip such as ketchup THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user selected this option in the observed front-laptop meal. IF dining context includes a social partner on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a warm sauced savory entree THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user selected perpendicular skewering in the observed left-social meal. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right OR working on a laptop on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is cold fruit or fruit dessert THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user selected perpendicular skewering in both observed right-attention fruit meals. IF dining context includes a social partner on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food includes bananas, brownies, or chocolate sauce THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user selected perpendicular skewering in the observed right-social meal.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a simple savory meal without sauces or dips THEN no BECAUSE the user skipped confirmation in repeated front-TV straightforward savory meals, favoring lower interaction burden when bite acquisition is likely easy. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a sauced mixed dish, bite-sized sandwiches, or a dipped savory meal such as fish bites with tartar sauce THEN yes BECAUSE the user requested explicit bite-acquisition confirmation in those front-TV meals, indicating extra verification is preferred when bite quality may be less predictable or messier. IF dining context includes working on a laptop in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a savory meal with a dip or sauce such as ketchup THEN yes BECAUSE the user selected confirmation in the observed front-laptop dipped lunch. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left OR working on a laptop on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food includes sauce, dip, dessert items with potentially messier or less predictable acquisition, or bite-sized sandwiches THEN yes BECAUSE the user explicitly requested confirmation in those observed left-attention meals. IF dining context includes a social partner on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a warm sauced savory entree THEN yes BECAUSE the user selected confirmation in the observed left-social meal. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is simple fresh fruit pieces THEN no BECAUSE the user skipped confirmation in the observed left-TV fruit meal. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right OR working on a laptop on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is simple cold fruit or fruit-and-cream items THEN no BECAUSE the user skipped confirmation in both observed right-attention fruit meals. IF dining context includes a social partner on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food includes bananas, brownies, or chocolate sauce THEN no BECAUSE the user skipped confirmation in the observed right-social meal.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user cannot lean forward due to lack of trunk control, so outside-mouth transfer is not physically compatible.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN not applicable BECAUSE the user's physical abilities support inside-mouth transfer rather than outside-mouth transfer.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a simple savory meal without sauces or dips THEN LED BECAUSE the user selected LED-only signaling in repeated front-TV straightforward meals. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a sauced mixed dish, bite-sized sandwiches, or a dipped savory meal such as fish bites with tartar sauce THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user selected a more salient multimodal cue in those front-TV meals, suggesting added prompting when the meal is more complex or requires more careful transfer timing. IF dining context includes working on a laptop in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a savory meal with a dip or sauce such as ketchup THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user selected multimodal readiness signaling in the observed front-laptop dipped lunch. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left OR working on a laptop on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food includes sauce, dip, dessert, cold fruit, or bite-sized sandwich items THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected multimodal readiness cues in left-attention contexts. IF dining context includes a social partner on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a warm sauced savory entree THEN LED BECAUSE the user selected LED-only readiness signaling in the observed left-social meal. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right OR working on a laptop on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is simple cold fruit or fruit dessert THEN LED BECAUSE the user selected LED-only signaling in both observed right-attention fruit meals. IF dining context includes a social partner on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food includes bananas, brownies, or chocolate sauce THEN LED BECAUSE the user selected LED-only readiness signaling in the observed right-social meal.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes a social partner in front of the user AND affective state is neutral AND food has no special sauce/dip relevance THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user explicitly selected low-effort automated initiation in that context. IF dining context includes watching TV in front OR working on a laptop in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is simple, dipped, or sauced THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected automated initiation in front-attention meals, including the new front-TV dipped savory meal. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left OR working on a laptop on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food includes sauce, dip, dessert, cold fruit, or bite-sized sandwich items THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user also selected automated initiation in those divided-attention meals. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right OR working on a laptop on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is simple cold fruit or fruit dessert THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user again selected automated initiation in both observed right-attention meals. IF dining context includes a social partner on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food includes bananas, brownies, or chocolate sauce THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected automated initiation in the observed right-social meal. IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN not button BECAUSE physical button use is incompatible with the user's severe upper-limb paralysis.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes watching TV in front OR working on a laptop in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is simple, dipped, or sauced THEN yes BECAUSE the user selected retracting in repeated front-attention meals, including the new front-TV dipped savory meal, indicating willingness to accept longer meal time to preserve visibility of an important forward visual target. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left OR working on a laptop on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food includes sauce, dip, dessert, cold fruit, or bite-sized sandwich items THEN yes BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose retracting in left-attention contexts. IF dining context includes a social partner on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a warm sauced savory entree THEN yes BECAUSE the user selected retracting in the observed left-social meal. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right OR working on a laptop on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a fruit dessert or simple cold fruit meal THEN yes BECAUSE the user selected retracting in both observed right-attention meals. IF dining context includes a social partner on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food includes bananas, brownies, or chocolate sauce THEN no BECAUSE the user selected no retraction in the observed right-social meal, showing that right-side social presence does not always outweigh the user's general preference to keep meals efficient.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF food has no sauces or dips AND any dining context AND any affective state THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping is not relevant. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a sauced mixed dish without a separate dip THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user selected no dipping for that entree, indicating that sauce on the food does not imply a desire for added dipping. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food includes a separate dip such as tartar sauce THEN less BECAUSE the user selected light dipping in the new front-TV dipped savory meal, showing a controlled-dipping preference rather than no dipping or heavy dipping when a separate dip is available. IF dining context includes a social partner on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a sauced savory entree without a separate dip THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user selected no dipping in the observed left-social meal, reinforcing that sauce already on the food does not create a dipping preference. IF dining context includes watching TV in front OR watching TV on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is bite-sized sandwiches without a separate dip THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user selected no dipping for sandwich meals when no separate dip was part of the meal. IF dining context includes working on a laptop in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food includes a separate dip such as ketchup THEN less BECAUSE the user selected light dipping in the observed front-laptop meal, suggesting a preference for controlled, less messy dipping while working. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context includes watching TV on the left OR working on a laptop on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown THEN less BECAUSE the user explicitly preferred light dipping rather than heavy dipping in observed sauce-containing left-attention meals. IF food is strawberries with whipped cream or another lightly dippable dessert AND dining context includes watching TV on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown THEN less BECAUSE the user selected light dipping in the observed right-TV fruit dessert meal. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left OR working on a laptop on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is fresh fruit without dip or sauce THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user selected no dipping in observed cold-fruit meals in those contexts. IF dining context includes a social partner on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food includes chocolate sauce or another dessert dip THEN less BECAUSE the user selected light dipping in the observed right-social meal, reinforcing a controlled-dipping tendency.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food has no sauces or dips THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user selected the shortest wait in repeated front-TV straightforward savory meals, including the sandwich meal. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a sauced mixed dish THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user selected a very long wait in that front-TV sauced meal, indicating more time to intervene is preferred when the meal is messier or more complex. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a dipped savory meal such as fish bites with tartar sauce THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user selected the shortest wait in the new front-TV dipped meal, showing that not all dip-associated meals trigger a long timeout. IF dining context includes working on a laptop in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a savory meal with a dip or sauce such as ketchup THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user selected a very long wait in the observed front-laptop dipped lunch, suggesting that working while managing a messier meal increases desire for intervention time. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left OR working on a laptop on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food includes sauce, dip, dessert, or bite-sized sandwich items that divide attention or may need intervention THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user explicitly selected a very long wait in those observed left-attention meals. IF dining context includes a social partner on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a warm sauced savory entree THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the user selected an intermediate wait in the observed left-social meal. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is simple fresh fruit pieces THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user selected the shortest wait in the observed left-TV fruit breakfast. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right OR working on a laptop on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a simple cold fruit or fruit-and-cream dessert THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user selected the shortest wait in both observed right-attention meals. IF dining context includes a social partner on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food includes bananas, brownies, or chocolate sauce THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user selected the shortest wait in the observed right-social meal.
